fills.note: Standard Ruth BCD ref; reviewer confirms canonical pin.

fills.value_json: {"_note":"Scenes S2-S3 shift to INTIMATE (whispered private exchange at midnight per MM Section 1); Boaz's blessing at 3:10a lifts to CEREMONIAL; the 3:13 oath stays INTIMATE on the P03 precedent.","scene_level":[{"scene_id":"S2","override_value":"INTIMATE"},{"scene_id":"S3","override_value":"INTIMATE"}],"moment_level":[{"verse":"3:10a","override_value":"CEREMONIAL"}]}
fills.note: MM multi-level register tagging drives this: pericope stays INFORMAL_CASUAL (NARRATIVE), S2/S3 INTIMATE, v.10 blessing CEREMONIAL, oath stays INTIMATE.
Order follows the 2.1 arc: execution → midnight recognition → petition → blessing → reassurance/pledge → the nearer-redeemer turn → oath.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM 2.1: Ruth goes down and does all her mother-in-law commanded — the plan carried out to the letter; no approved token names execution of a prior plan.
MM 2.1/3.9: Ruth asks Boaz to spread his wing over her — the marriage petition that overtakes the plan; no approved arc token names this petition.
MM 2.1: Boaz quiets her fear and pledges 'all that you say I will do for you'.
MM 2.1: 'there is a redeemer nearer than I' — the legal complication that hands the story to the gate; no approved token covers it.

fills.note: PRIOR_PERICOPE_CARRY_FORWARD = execution of P08's plan; PRIOR_ACTION_CONTEXT = the 2:12 wing, 2:20 redeemer, 2:1 chayil, 1:8 hesed callbacks; INSTITUTIONAL = kinsman-redeemer + gate legal venue; DIVINE = YHWH invoked in blessing and oath.
Scene 1 plain/economical quiet motion; the whispered exchange is INTIMATE and WEIGHTED with formulas; ends UNRESOLVED (which redeemer) yet STILLED (lie down until morning).
Scene 1 told fast (BRISK); the midnight startle RISES; the talk SLOWS and HOLDS on the gap between 'you are a redeemer' and 'nearer than I'; SETTLES into the protocol.
Closes the wing-image and the cross-pericope pairs; reactivates the assent-pattern; opens the nearer-redeemer legal question; stages the gate scene.
S1 Ruth executes Naomi's command; relational role retained — her petitioner function emerges in S2.
Unmarked in scene 1 — named plainly as Ruth.
S1 Boaz is the owner sleeping by his grain at the floor.
Unmarked — named as Boaz; the 'the man' naming begins at the startle (S2).
Naomi is the absent planner whose command Ruth carries out.
MM names her only relationally (חֲמוֹתָהּ / 'her mother-in-law') in this scene — a kinship-closure naming of the type the spec marks.
קְצֵה הָעֲרֵמָה / 'the end of the grain heap' — no registry code exists; proposed new PL for reviewer registration.
Executes Naomi's plan to the letter and stages the night; no approved scene_kind names a plan-execution/staging scene.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 1: Ruth 'does according to all that her mother-in-law commanded' and the night's stage is set; the dominant function is execution of a prior plan, not initiative or instruction.

fills.note: S2 Boaz is the startled sleeper Ruth petitions; relational anchor as the household's redeemer-kin.
MM explicitly marks: named only 'the man' (הָאִישׁ) at the startle; his name rests until his answer.
S2 Ruth names herself and asks for the wing — her scene function is petitioner.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 2: Ruth speaks past the plan to petition for the wing; relational tokens (DAUGHTER_IN_LAW) miss the load-bearing petition function the scene turns on.

fills.note: MM marks: 'a woman' to the startled man, then by her own mouth 'Ruth your servant' — amah, a step up from her shifchah of 2:13.
B18 is the redeemer-role Ruth names Boaz into — the ground of her request.
Role-being ('a redeemer'); no marked personal naming to record.
The startle, the question, and Ruth's appeal for the wing — APPEAL_SCENE fits the scene's spine.

Append: B2 Elimelech (the dead clansman whose line the redeemer-duty serves) and B3 Naomi (Ruth's mother-in-law, referenced via the kinship relation). ANCESTOR used for Elimelech as the household's deceased line-head.
S3 Boaz blesses, pledges, discloses, and swears as the household's redeemer-kin.
Unmarked — his name Boaz returns once he answers.
S3 Ruth is the one whose petition is being answered — petitioner carried from S2.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 3: Ruth is the petitioner whose request Boaz answers with blessing and pledge; same scene function as S2.

fills.note: MM marks: addressed twice as 'my daughter' (בִּתִּי) and praised as 'a woman of worth' (eshet chayil); the address form is the marked naming on the being entry.
YHWH invoked in the blessing and sworn by in the oath.
Named YHWH; no marked referential shift.
B19 is the nearer redeemer — a kinsman of Elimelech's line ahead of Boaz in the legal queue.
MM marks: unnamed here — only 'a redeemer nearer than I' (registry B19 form).
B21 = all the gate of Boaz's people, the public whose knowledge of Ruth's worth he cites.
MM emphasizes 'all the gate of my people knows' — the collective naming carries the gate-legal-venue weight (CB_0006); marked. Reviewer may judge this as too marked if tempering is preferred.
'The young men' Ruth did not go after — the marriage alternative she declined, the measure of her hesed.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 3: 'the young men, whether poor or rich' are present only as the suitors Ruth chose against; no approved role names this declined-alternative function.

fills.note: Plain collective ('the young men, poor or rich'); not a map-attention-called naming.
Surface form הַבַּחוּרִים / 'the young men' — MM 3A leaves this group deliberately uncoded (no wikilink). Coded B? per drafter rule.

Boaz answers the petition with blessing, honor, pledge, disclosure, and oath; BLESSING_SCENE and VOW_SCENE each name only one act, so a mint best captures the binding response. Reviewer may prefer BLESSING_SCENE.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 3: the scene's spine is Boaz's binding pledge ('all that you say I will do'), sealed under oath and conditioned on the nearer redeemer; no single approved scene_kind covers the composite response.

fills.note: Append B2 Elimelech — the dead clansman whose line the redeemer-queue serves (referenced in the redeemer relationships).
Ruth descends to the floor and does all commanded; no approved descent kind.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P1 (3:6): Ruth 'goes down' (vatered) to the threshing floor; the approved set has MIGRATED/DEPARTED/ARRIVED_AT but no descent kind, and the compliance beat is carried in slots.

fills.note: Compliance with Naomi's command preserved as a slot.
FIG_0141 heart-good-after-meal idiom preserved as disposition slot.
Boaz reclines at the grain-heap's end; no approved reclining kind.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P3 (3:7b): Boaz lies down (vayyishkav) at the end of the grain heap; the approved set has SAT/ROSE/DWELT_AT but no reclining kind.

fills.note: He lies down after eating and being content.
The load-bearing act (CB_0042) — Ruth uncovers the place of his feet and lies down.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P4 (3:7c): the uncovering of the place of his feet (gillah margelotav) is the scene's load-bearing act; no approved kind names it.

fills.note: Three narrated beats decomposed into a components array (came softly / uncovered / lay down).

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P4: gillah margelotav — uncovering the place of his feet; no approved action covers it (PLACED/POSITIONED do not fit).
MM P4: vatishkav — she lies down; no approved reclining action exists.

fills.note: The midnight startle (FIG_0130 vayyecherad); no approved startle kind.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P5 (3:8a): vayyecherad ha-ish — the man trembles/is startled at midnight; the force is kept whole as its own kind.

fills.note: Midnight time anchor and the two startle beats preserved; 'the man' naming carried as referential_form.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P5: vayyecherad — he trembled/was startled; no approved action names the startle.
MM P5: vayyilafet — he twisted/writhed about; TURNED_BACK does not fit the startled writhe.

fills.note: The uncovering at his feet wakes/startles him.
'and behold, a woman' — perception with the hinneh attention-marker.
Boaz's information-seeking question 'who are you'.
Ruth self-identifies: 'I am Ruth your servant'.
FIG_0132 amah-vs-shifchah status shift preserved as a slot.
Answer paired with the question it answers.
The petition: spread your wing over your servant.
CB_0037/FIG_0131 wing-of-refuge petition; closes the 2:12 wing as a marriage request.
The ground of the request: you are a redeemer — the legal word in Ruth's own mouth.
CB_0001 — the withheld 'redeemer' word enters Ruth's mouth here.
Grounds the petition of P9.
'Blessed are you of YHWH, my daughter.'
Worked-pair blessing slot idiom; CB_0008/FIG_0137. CEREMONIAL register moment (3:10a).
Answers the petition first with blessing.
Her last hesed better than the first.
CB_0011/FIG_0133/FIG_0111 — third station of the hesed thread.
What she had not done: gone after the young men.
'The young men' kept as B? (deliberately uncoded group); poor-or-rich scope preserved.
Elaborates the measure of the hesed in P12.
Reassurance + pledge — 'do not fear; all that you say I will do for you.'
FIG_0123/FIG_0136 — the pledge gives back Ruth's own 3:5 assent words.
Pledge answers the petition.
The reason: all the gate of my people knows she is a woman of worth.
CB_0032/CB_0006/FIG_0134 — completes the chayil pair with 2:1 and names the gate venue.
Gives the reason for the pledge of P14.
Boaz owns the role: truly I am a redeemer.
Discloses the complication: a redeemer nearer than I.
CB_0001/FIG_0138/FIG_0112 — turns 2:20's comfort into a legal queue.
Qualifies his own redeemer-claim.
Command: lodge the night — encoded as DIRECTED + commanded_step per action-idiom ruling.
FIG_0140 reverse-handoff: he now sets her next move.
The morning protocol stated as a conditional plan.
CB_0001/FIG_0140 — the conditional queue, sealed by the oath in P20.
The protocol follows from the disclosed nearer redeemer.
The chai-YHWH oath sealing the promise.
FIG_0135 — oath stays INTIMATE register per P03 precedent.
Seals the morning protocol.
Command: lie down until the morning — DIRECTED + commanded_step.
FIG_0139 lie-down-authority-transfer.
Closes the night; pairs with the 'lodge the night' instruction of P18.

Kept all 21 MM-granularity propositions. Multi-beat propositions were decomposed inside event_specific_slots via *_components arrays rather than splitting prop_ids: P4 (came softly / uncovered / lay down), P5 (trembled / twisted), P14 (do-not-fear / pledge), P18 (the two conditional protocol branches). Two times (TM_HALF_OF_THE_NIGHT, TM_NIGHT_UNTIL_MORNING) and one place (PL_END_OF_GRAIN_HEAP) are proposed registry IDs for reviewer confirmation.

remarks: P09 threshing-floor night. Register: pericope INFORMAL_CASUAL (NARRATIVE); S2/S3 INTIMATE, v.10 blessing CEREMONIAL, oath INTIMATE per MM. New registry IDs proposed (reviewer to confirm/register): place PL_END_OF_GRAIN_HEAP; times TM_HALF_OF_THE_NIGHT and TM_NIGHT_UNTIL_MORNING; being B? for 'the young men' (left uncoded in the MM). Mints declared: arc_elements PLAN_EXECUTION/WING_PETITION/REASSURANCE_PLEDGE/NEARER_REDEEMER_DISCLOSURE; scene_kinds PLAN_EXECUTION_SCENE and PLEDGE_SCENE; roles PETITIONER and FOREGONE_SUITORS; proposition_kinds WENT_DOWN/LAY_DOWN/UNCOVERED/TREMBLED; actions UNCOVERED/LAY_DOWN/TREMBLED/TWISTED. Two judgment calls flagged for review: (1) PLEDGE_SCENE for S3 over approved BLESSING_SCENE/VOW_SCENE, since the scene's spine is the binding pledge under oath; (2) the marked referential_form ALL_THE_GATE_OF_MY_PEOPLE on B21 may be over-marked if the reviewer prefers tempering, though the gate-legal-venue weight (CB_0006) argues for it. Cross-pericope figure pairs (FIG_0131/0132/0111/0123/0134/0112) close here per the MM; those are compiled cross_ref data, untouched.
